required: Which mechanisms specifically weak? (SN1/SN2? Elimination? Addition?)

effortModel.citation: Retake = ~70-75% of first-time speed; CPA retake 40-60% shorter confirmed; orgo retake Reddit patterns

expectedStrategy: Day 1-9: 50% scan reading (known content fast), 40% mechanism drill (stop and draw), 10% blind recall self-test
Day 10-13: mechanism drill by type — SN1/SN2/E1/E2/addition/oxidation
Day 14: timed mock test under exam conditions
warnings: 'Familiarity illusion' risk: reading feels easy but drawing mechanisms on exam is different skill
Mechanism drill must be ≥40% of time — failure reason from last attempt
alternatives: if specific weak mechanisms identified: prioritize those chapters first (not Ch.10→15 linear order)
if very strong in some chapters: scan-only for those, full drill only for weak chapters
hardFailChecks: must NOT give same plan as TC-33 (freshman) — different speed, different focus
must ask specifically which mechanism type is weak
must NOT start with Ch.10 linear reading — weak mechanism chapters should come first
toneExpectation: frustration-validating; past failure reframed as strategic guide; action-focused; 'last time failed → this time drill' narrative
